question: What are common failure modes in multi-agent systems?

heuristic_evaluation.question: What are common failure modes in multi-agent systems?

claim_lines: Common failure modes in multi-agent systems cluster around coordination, role assignment, verification, and evaluation. Recent survey and benchmark evidence shows that adding more agents does not automatically improve performance: coordination costs rise, errors can propagate across message chains, and groups can reach agreement without reaching the right answer. https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s44336-024-00009-2 https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.01045 https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.04474

- coordination overhead — Surveys and Silo-Bench both report that communication/coordination cost grows with scale and can erase parallelization gains: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s44336-024-00009-2 https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.01045
- role confusion — Role-specification failure is a major failure mode; role overstepping means an agent behaves like another role: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.02770
- error propagation — Minor inaccuracies can propagate through message dependencies and become system-level false consensus: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.04474

- consensus without correctness — Agents may coordinate and exchange information yet still fail to synthesize a correct answer; Silo-Bench explicitly names consensus failure: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.01045

- evaluation difficulty — Reliable evaluation needs annotated traces, human agreement checks, LLM-as-a-Judge pipelines, and split strategies for multi-turn workflows: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657 https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/agent-framework/agents/evaluation

coverage_audit.reason: Clearly identifies rising communication/coordination cost as scale increases.
Explicitly addresses role-specification failure and role overstepping.
States that small inaccuracies can propagate through message chains into system-level errors.
Directly notes agreement can occur without the correct answer.
Explains that multi-agent evaluation is hard and requires traces, checks, and workflow-specific methods.

citation_audit.claim: adding more agents does not automatically improve performance: coordination costs rise, errors can propagate across message chains, and groups can reach agreement without reaching the right answer
citation_audit.reason: This is a high-level synthesis that is plausibly supported by the cited survey/benchmarks.

citation_audit.claim: coordination/communication cost grows with scale and can erase parallelization gains
citation_audit.reason: Directly aligned with coordination overhead as a failure mode.

citation_audit.claim: role-specification failure is a major failure mode; role overstepping means an agent behaves like another role
citation_audit.reason: Plausibly supported by a role consistency / role overstepping paper.

citation_audit.claim: Minor inaccuracies can propagate through message dependencies and become system-level false consensus
citation_audit.reason: Fits an error-cascade or false-consensus analysis.

citation_audit.claim: Agents may coordinate and exchange information yet still fail to synthesize a correct answer; Silo-Bench explicitly names consensus failure
citation_audit.reason: Directly matches the requested consensus-without-correctness aspect.

citation_audit.claim: Reliable evaluation needs annotated traces, human agreement checks, LLM-as-a-Judge pipelines, and split strategies for multi-turn workflows
citation_audit.reason: This is a reasonable description of evaluation difficulty and methods for agent systems.

source_audit.reason: Relevant empirical/taxonomy-style research source for evaluation and failure analysis.
Benchmark-style source directly relevant to distributed coordination and consensus failures.
Relevant empirical failure analysis for error propagation and false consensus.
Official framework documentation is relevant for evaluation practice, though not a research source.
Likely a relevant survey article, but the metadata provided is too sparse to judge stronger.

judge_evaluation.summary: The answer covers all required failure modes with generally plausible citations and useful engineering framing, though some evidence claims are broad and the uncertainty handling is modest.
judge_evaluation.recommended_fix: Tighten the source-to-claim linkage by naming the specific findings each citation supports, and add one or two concrete examples of each failure mode in practice.
